title: Incorrect variable reference in custom JS code
symptom: The custom JS function returns 'URL not found' when trying to extract a URL from a string containing a WeWeb variable.
applies_when: Using a regex to extract a URL
Inserting WeWeb variable into JS code
not_this_if: The variable is correctly referenced without quotes
root_cause: The issue arises because the variable is enclosed in quotes, making it a string literal instead of a reference to the actual variable. This prevents the regex from processing the intended variable value.
fix_steps: Remove the quotes around the variable in the JS code
Ensure the variable is referenced correctly without quotes
Test the function again to confirm it returns the expected URL
agent_instruction: Remove quotes around the WeWeb variable in your JS code to reference it correctly.
